body.title: [sprint] devstral@Hex dispatch times out (~120s) mid-loop — now the critical-path wall for DS.0 edits
body.intention: I intend that DS.0's architect can finish planning without a Hex inference timeout killing it mid-loop, because after the loop-shape redesign the inference dispatch timeout is now the thing blocking edits.
body.description:
**Problem:** the 2026-07-04 post-redesign confirm-by (T-coding-redesign-observe-edits-then-close) showed the redesign machinery working end-to-end — signature map (8 files/2653 chars), architect/editor split, windowed 100-line reads all engaged live — but the ARCHITECT hit a devstral@Hex dispatch TIMEOUT (121s) on turn 12 (after 11 productive turns: 8 windowed Reads + 3 Bash), before producing a plan. The honest-halt fix correctly fired (availability-midrun-wall-halt, no blind retry — validated T-ds-loop-no-identical-retry live), but the editor phase never ran, so edits stayed 0 for an INFRASTRUCTURE reason, not a loop-shape reason. The wall moved from 'loop shape' to 'inference latency/timeout'.
**Observed:** turns ran ~20-25s each, then turn 12 exceeded the hardcoded 120s InferenceRequest timeout (agentic_loop.py timeout=120). Likely accumulated context (history window = 10 groups x ~4KB windowed reads + signature map + system) made devstral's generation exceed 120s on the M1 Max.
**Proposed shape (investigate + pick):** (a) raise the dispatch timeout for FLAT-RATE local sources (Hex/ollama don't pay per second — a longer timeout is free) — parameterize timeout by source billing type; and/or (b) reduce architect context pressure: the architect read 8 files DESPITE the signature map — lean the architect prompt harder on the map so it reads far less (the map exists to REPLACE reading, not supplement it); and/or (c) profile whether devstral@Hex is simply slow on ~12-15K-token prompts and the num_ctx/model choice needs revisiting.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/agentic_loop.py (timeout=120 hardcode), the architect prompt in architect_editor.py, possibly source/timeout config.
**Design rules:** log the crossing; proof-on-close; NO SQLITE.
**Scope boundary:** IN — diagnose the mid-loop timeout + make the architect able to finish planning within the inference budget on Hex. OUT — the full edit-conversion confirm (re-run the observe after this).
**Test plan:** a re-run observe shows the architect reaching DONE (a plan) without a mid-loop timeout, handing off to the editor. (Timeout-parameterization has a unit test; the latency fix is observe-confirmed.)
**Completion criteria:** the architect completes its plan on devstral@Hex without a mid-loop dispatch timeout on a real multi-file ticket (observe evidence), OR the timeout is shown to be a genuine model-latency ceiling requiring a stronger/faster planner (documented).
**Rollback:** timeout parameterization is additive; revert restores the 120s hardcode.

**Why this is the critical path now:** the loop-shape redesign shipped+proven (3 levers); this timeout is the next wall between DS.0 and a real edit.

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
